briefs.title: BC Tightens EV Range Rules and Credit System for Auto Suppliers Starting 2026
briefs.summary: BC updated its Zero-Emission Vehicles Regulation, raising the minimum range required for battery and fuel-cell EVs from 241 km to 150 km, setting new range rules for plug-in hybrids by model year, and changing how ZEV credits (government-issued points for selling zero-emission vehicles) are issued from October 2026.
briefs.action_required: Review your ZEV supply agreements and credit applications to ensure vehicles meet the new range thresholds before October 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC ZEV credit issuance rule change takes effect — new range thresholds apply
